answer:
# Audit Planning Deliverables

---

# Engagement Strategy

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| Client Background | Not available in provided document/context — no uploaded financial statement or entity-specific information provided |
| Financial Statement Context | Not available in provided document/context |
| Engagement Scope | Audit of financial statements in accordance with ISA 300 and applicable ISAs; scope to be defined upon receipt of entity-specific information [S1] |
| Applicable Reporting/Audit Considerations | ISA 300 (Planning), ISA 220 (Revised) (Quality Management), ISA 210 (Engagement Terms), ISA 315 (Revised 2019) (Risk Assessment), ISA 330 (Responses to Assessed Risks) [S1] |
| Key Audit Focus Areas | To be determined following risk assessment procedures; high-risk areas to receive deployment of experienced team members and increased audit budget hours [S2] |
| Planned Audit Approach | Risk-based audit approach; overall audit strategy to set scope, timing and direction; audit plan to detail nature, timing and extent of procedures at assertion level [S1] |
| Initial vs. Recurring Engagement | If initial engagement: communication with predecessor auditor required; review of prior period financial statements and opening balances mandatory [S4] |
| Management Coordination | Elements of planning may be discussed with management to coordinate procedures; overall strategy and plan remain the auditor's sole responsibility [S2] |

---

# Planning Memorandum

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| Audit Objective | To plan and perform the audit so that it will be conducted in an effective manner, enabling the auditor to express an opinion on the financial statements [S3] |
| Scope | Audit of the complete set of financial statements for the relevant reporting period; scope characteristics to be identified per ISA 300 paragraph 8(a) [S1] |
| Reporting Framework | Not available in provided document/context — applicable financial reporting framework to be confirmed per engagement letter requirements [S4] |
| Significant Account Areas | Not available in provided document/context — to be identified following risk assessment and understanding of the entity |
| Audit Approach | Risk-based; risk assessment procedures performed early; further audit procedures (tests of controls and substantive procedures) designed in response to assessed risks [S1] |
| Timeline | Planning to commence at or shortly after completion of the previous audit; interim and final audit stages to be scheduled; key cutoff dates to be identified [S1] |
| Management Responsibilities | Preparation of financial statements; maintenance of internal controls; provision of information and access to personnel as agreed in the engagement letter [S4] |
| Audit Team Responsibilities | Engagement partner to review overall audit strategy and audit plan; key team members to be involved in planning; direction, supervision and review to be documented [S1] |

---

# Materiality Calculation

> **No uploaded financial statement data is available.** The calculations below present the standard methodology framework to be applied once entity-specific financial data is provided.

| Benchmark | Source Value | Percentage Applied | Calculated Materiality |
|---|---|---|---|
| Profit Before Tax | Not available in provided document/context | 5% – 10% (typical range) | To be calculated upon receipt of financial data |
| Revenue | Not available in provided document/context | 0.5% – 1% (typical range) | To be calculated upon receipt of financial data |
| Total Assets | Not available in provided document/context | 1% – 2% (typical range) | To be calculated upon receipt of financial data |
| **Recommended Overall Materiality** | Not available in provided document/context | Selected benchmark % | To be determined |
| **Performance Materiality** | Not available in provided document/context | 60% – 75% of overall materiality | To be determined |
| **Clearly Trivial Threshold** | Not available in provided document/context | 3% – 5% of overall materiality | To be determined |
| **Rationale for Selected Benchmark** | To be documented once financial data is available | Benchmark selected based on entity type, profitability, and user focus | To be documented in audit file per ISA 320 |

---

# Risk Assessment

| Risk Area | Source Fact | Risk Description | Risk Level | Planned Audit Response |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Management Override / Fraud Risk | ISA 240 referenced in ISA 300 [S2] | Risk that management overrides controls to manipulate financial results; present in all audits as a presumed risk | High | Unpredictable audit procedures; journal entry testing; review of accounting estimates for bias |
| Revenue Recognition / Cutoff | Not available in provided document/context | Risk of premature or incorrect revenue recognition around period end | To be assessed | Cutoff testing; analytical procedures on revenue trends; substantive testing of transactions near year-end |
| Inventory Valuation | Not available in provided document/context | Risk of overstatement of inventory due to obsolescence or incorrect costing | To be assessed | Attendance at physical inventory count; test of cost vs. net realisable value; review of slow-moving items |
| Trade Receivables Recoverability | Not available in provided document/context | Risk that receivables are overstated due to inadequate provisioning for doubtful debts | To be assessed | Aged debtor analysis; subsequent receipts testing; review of provisioning methodology |
| Related Party Transactions | Not available in provided document/context | Risk of undisclosed or non-arm's-length related party transactions affecting completeness and accuracy | To be assessed | Inquiry of management; review of board minutes; inspection of contracts; confirmation of terms |
| VAT / Corporate Tax Compliance | Not available in provided document/context | Risk of misstatement in tax balances or non-compliance with tax regulations | To be assessed | Review of tax computations; reconciliation of tax charge to accounting profit; review of deferred tax |
| Going Concern | Not available in provided document/context | Risk that the entity may not be able to continue as a going concern, affecting basis of preparation | To be assessed | Review of cash flow forecasts; analysis of debt covenants; inquiry of management regarding future plans |
| Opening Balances (if initial engagement) | ISA for LCE 4.5.2–4.5.4 [S4] | Risk that opening balances contain misstatements materially affecting current period financial statements | To be assessed | Inspect predecessor auditor's working papers; perform specific procedures on opening balances; assess consistency of accounting policies |

---

# Audit Programs

**Inventory**
- Attend or observe physical inventory count at period end; test count procedures and reconcile to records
- Test inventory valuation: agree costs to supplier invoices or cost records; compare to net realisable value
- Review aged inventory listing for slow-moving or obsolete items; assess adequacy of write-down provisions
- Perform cut-off testing on goods received and dispatched around year-end

**Revenue**
- Perform analytical procedures on revenue by period, product/service line, and customer
- Test a sample of revenue transactions for completeness, accuracy, and correct period recognition (cutoff)
- Review credit notes issued after year-end for evidence of period-end overstatement
- Agree revenue to underlying contracts, delivery documentation, and cash receipts

**Trade Receivables**
- Obtain and review aged debtors listing; agree total to general ledger
- Perform direct confirmation of significant balances with customers
- Test subsequent receipts for balances outstanding at year-end
- Assess adequacy of the bad debt provision; review management's provisioning methodology

**Related Parties**
- Obtain management's list of related parties; compare to prior year and board minutes
- Inspect contracts and agreements with related parties for arm's-length terms
- Review bank statements and ledger entries for transactions with related parties not disclosed
- Confirm completeness of related party disclosures in the financial statements

**Tax / VAT**
- Agree the current tax charge to the tax computation; reconcile effective tax rate to statutory rate
- Review deferred tax balances for completeness and accuracy; assess recoverability of deferred tax assets
- Review VAT returns for the period; reconcile VAT output/input to revenue and purchases
- Assess any outstanding tax assessments or disputes for appropriate disclosure and provisioning

**Going Concern**
- Obtain and critically review management's going concern assessment and supporting cash flow forecasts
- Assess key assumptions in forecasts for reasonableness; compare to historical performance
- Review compliance with debt covenants and terms of borrowing facilities
- Inquire of management regarding any post-balance-sheet events affecting the entity's ability to continue

**Financial Statement Close and Disclosures**
- Agree financial statement line items to the trial balance and supporting schedules
- Review journal entries, particularly manual and late entries, for unusual items
- Assess completeness and accuracy of disclosures against the applicable financial reporting framework
- Review accounting policies for appropriateness and consistency with prior periods [S4]

**Opening Balances (Initial Engagement)**
- Communicate with predecessor auditor and, where permitted, inspect prior year working papers [S4]
- Confirm that prior period closing balances have been correctly brought forward [S4]
- Perform specific audit procedures to obtain evidence on opening balances where prior year was unaudited [S4]
- Evaluate the effect of any prior year modified opinion on current period risk assessment [S4]

---

# Staffing Recommendations

| Role | Responsibility | Estimated Effort / Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Engagement Partner | Review and approve overall audit strategy and audit plan; sign audit report; oversee quality management per ISA 220 (Revised) [S1] | Focused involvement at planning, key risk areas, and completion stages |
| Audit Manager | Coordinate day-to-day execution of the audit plan; direct and supervise senior and assistant auditors; review working papers [S2] | Continuous involvement throughout fieldwork and reporting |
| Senior Auditor | Lead fieldwork on high-risk and complex audit areas (e.g., revenue, receivables, tax); perform risk assessment procedures | Higher effort allocation to high-risk areas per overall audit strategy [S2] |
| Assistant Auditor | Execute routine substantive procedures (e.g., inventory counts, confirmations, vouching); prepare working papers | Effort proportionate to volume of transactions and account areas |
| Tax / Compliance Specialist | Address complex tax computations, deferred tax, VAT compliance, and any regulatory matters | Deployed as expert for complex tax matters per ISA 300 resource planning [S1] |
| IT Audit Specialist (if applicable) | Assess IT general controls and application controls relevant to financial reporting systems | To be determined based on entity's IT environment |
| Review Intensity | High-risk areas (fraud, revenue, related parties) to receive engagement partner and manager review; lower-risk areas reviewed by manager only [S2] | Documented in audit plan per ISA 300 paragraph 9(a) [S1] |
| Estimated Effort by Risk Area | High-risk areas (fraud, revenue, going concern): highest budget hours; moderate-risk (receivables, tax, inventory): medium hours; lower-risk (disclosures, routine balances): lower hours | To be quantified in audit budget once entity financial data and risk assessment are finalised [S2] |

citations.text: (Ref: Para. A5)   Preliminary Engagement Activities   6. The auditor shall undertake the following activities at the beginning of the current audit engagement:   (a) Performing procedures required by ISA 220 (Revised) regarding the acceptance and continuance of the client relationship and the specific audit engagement;1   (b) Evaluating compliance with relevant ethical requirements, including those related to independence, in accordance with ISA 220 (Revised),2 and   (c) Establishing an understanding of the terms of the engagement, as required by ISA 210.3 (Ref: Para. A6–A8)   Planning Activities 7. The auditor shall establish an overall audit strategy that sets the scope, timing and direction of the audit, and that guides the   development of the audit plan.   1 ISA 220 (Revised), Quality Management for an Audit of Financial Statements, paragraphs 22–24 2 ISA 220 (Revised), paragraphs 16–21 3 ISA 210, Agreeing the Terms of Audit Engagements, paragraphs 9–13PLANNING AN AUDIT OF FINANCI

The auditor may decide to discuss elements of planning with the entity’s management to help the auditor manage and achieve quality at the engagement level (for example, to coordinate some of the planned audit procedures with the work of the entity’s personnel). Although these discussions often occur, the overall audit strategy and the audit plan remain the auditor’s responsibility. When discussing matters included in the overall audit strategy or audit plan, care is required in order not to compromise the effectiveness of the audit. For example, discussing the nature and timing of detailed audit procedures with management may compromise the effectiveness of the audit by making the audit procedures too predictable.   Involvement of Key Engagement Team Members (Ref: Para. 5)   A5. The involvement of the engagement partner and other key members of the engagement team in planning the audit draws on their experience and insight, thereby enhancing the effectiveness and efficiency of the plan

A25. In some circumstances, those charged with governance may wish to be made aware of the details of other deficiencies in internal control the auditor has communicated to management, or be briefly informed of the nature of the other deficiencies. Alternatively, the auditor may consider it appropriate to inform those charged with governance of the communication of the other deficiencies to management. In either case, the auditor may report orally or in writing to those charged with governance as appropriate.   A26. ISA 260 (Revised) establishes relevant considerations regarding communication with those charged with governance when all of them are involved in managing the entity.11   Considerations Specific to Public Sector Entities (Ref: Para. 9–10)   A27. Public sector auditors may have additional responsibilities to communicate deficiencies in internal control that the auditor has identified during the audit, in ways, at a level of detail and to parties not envisaged in this ISA. Fo

Initial Audit Engagements   4.5.1. If the engagement is an initial audit and there has been a change in auditor, the auditor shall communicate with the predecessor auditor, in compliance with relevant ethical requirements.   4.5.2. The auditor shall read the most recent financial statements, if any, and the auditor’s report thereon, if any, for information relevant to opening balances, including disclosures.THE INTERNATIONAL STANDARD ON AUDITING FOR AUDITS OF FINANCIAL STATEMENTS OF LESS COMPLEX ENTITIES   THE ISA FOR LCE 32   4.5.3. If the prior period’s financial statements were audited by a predecessor auditor and there was a modification to the opinion, the auditor shall evaluate the effect of the matter giving rise to the modification in assessing the risks of material misstatement in the current period’s financial statements.9   4.5.4.
